mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate and tightening financial conditions, so higher policy rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in most periods when FEDFUNDS is rising over a 3-month window, or the sign of the rolling 2-year correlation is persistently positive, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should reduce expected earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the average 60-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, consumer spending outpaces housing supply growth and supports broad nominal demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns positive while HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not improve 20-day SPX returns relative to either proxy alone, or if the conditional hit rate is below 50% over a rolling 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average when INDPRO is above its prior-month level, or if the signal's directional accuracy stays below 52% for any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDPC1 confirms real final demand is expanding, so equities should reprice higher over the next quarter as earnings expectations rise and recession risk falls.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP growth fails to align with positive 60-day SPX returns in the majority of expansion quarters, or if the sign relationship reverses for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL runs above the policy rate, real rates become more negative and monetary policy is effectively easier, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy-rate condition does not correspond to higher 20-day SPX returns than matched control periods, or if the spread has no incremental explanatory power beyond CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS with no offsetting drop in UNRATE signals weakening payroll momentum and rising recession probability, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below its 3-month moving average while UNRATE is flat or higher
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor weakness condition is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the combined signal is indistinguishable from noise in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing slowdown regime, weaker HOUST signals cooling interest-sensitive demand and future earnings pressure, so further declines in housing starts should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns after the housing-starts drawdown are not below unconditional returns, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is held constant in out-of-sample tests.
